title: Motion to Bring SB13 to Senate Floor Coming Tomorrow! Email Your Senator!
author: James Silberman
orientation: A March 2020 action alert from Free the States announcing that an Oklahoma State Senator would file a motion the next day to pull SB13 (the Abolition of Abortion in Oklahoma Act) directly to the Senate floor after it was tabled in committee, urging abolitionists to email every senator before the vote.

questions.question: What procedural action was scheduled for the Oklahoma State Senate the day after this article was published?
questions.answer: A motion was set to be made on Wednesday to bring SB13 directly to the Senate floor for a hearing, with a competing motion to table it expected to receive an immediate vote.
questions.quote: A motion is going to be made in the State Senate on Wednesday to bring SB13 directly to the floor for a hearing. A motion to table this motion will likely be made by an establishment Republican and that motion to table will get a vote immediately.

questions.question: What was the practical stakes of Wednesday's vote?
questions.answer: The Senate would either vote to hear SB13 or to table it permanently, effectively deciding whether the bill survived the session.
questions.quote: So, on Wednesday, the Senate will vote on whether to hear SB13 or table it for good.

questions.question: What grassroots and institutional support did SB13 already have before this vote?
questions.answer: Three thousand people had rallied at the Capitol on February 11 for Abolition Day, abolitionists across Oklahoma had been continuously contacting senators, and the Oklahoma Baptists, Oklahoma Free Will Baptists, and Oklahoma Republican Party had all passed resolutions demanding the bill.
questions.quote: On February 11, three thousand people showed up to the State Capitol for the Abolition Day rally to demand SB13. Thousands of abolitionists from across the state have been constantly emailing and calling their state Senators asking them to support SB13. The Oklahoma Baptists, Oklahoma Free Will Baptists, and Oklahoma Republican Party all passed resolutions demanding SB13.

questions.question: Whom did the article name as responsible for blocking SB13 from being heard?
questions.answer: James Silberman names Senators Greg McCortney and Greg Treat as the men whose hardened hearts kept the bill from receiving a hearing, after it was tabled in committee and the committee deadline passed.
questions.quote: Yet because of Greg McCortney and Greg Treat hardening their hearts, the bill hasn’t even been heard. It was tabled in committee and the deadline for bills passing out of committee has passed.

questions.question: How can a bill reach the Senate floor in Oklahoma after missing the committee deadline?
questions.answer: Committee passage is not strictly required; a bill can be brought directly to the floor if a motion to do so is filed and a majority of the Senate votes in favor of hearing it.
questions.quote: A bill does not have to pass out of committee, however. It can be brought straight to the Senate floor if a motion to do so is filed and a majority of the Senate votes in favor of hearing the bill.

questions.question: What specific action did Free the States ask readers to take?
questions.answer: Readers were asked to email their own State Senator, signal that they were watching Wednesday's vote, frame a vote to table SB13 as a vote against abolition, and CC the rest of the Senate.
questions.quote: Please email your State Senator to let them know you’re watching to see what they do on Wednesday and that a vote to table SB13 is a vote against the abolition of abortion. If you don’t know who your State Senator is, find out [here](http://www.oksenate.gov/findmylegislature.aspx). CC the rest of the Senate in the email as well.

questions.question: How did the article frame a vote to table SB13 morally?
questions.answer: It framed any senator's vote to table the bill as equivalent to a vote against abolishing abortion, removing the procedural cover senators often use to avoid taking a recorded position.
questions.quote: a vote to table SB13 is a vote against the abolition of abortion

related.title: Senator McCortney Again Denies SB13 a Fair Hearing
related.reason: Direct backstory on McCortney tabling SB13 in committee, which is the procedural situation this floor motion was trying to bypass.

related.title: The Inexhaustible Cowardice of Oklahoma Senate President Greg Treat
related.reason: Profiles the other senator named in this article as responsible for keeping SB13 from a hearing.

related.title: Contrary to Treat's Media Tour, SB13 is Not a Secession Bill
related.reason: Rebuts Greg Treat's public arguments against SB13 in the same 2020 session window covered by this action alert.

related.title: Why "No Bill But SB13" ?
related.reason: Explains the strategic rationale behind insisting on SB13 specifically, which is the bill this email campaign was defending.

related.title: Anti-SB13 Campaigners Aren't Ignorant. They're Trying to Deceive You.
related.reason: Equips activists with counter-arguments to the establishment opposition the article warns will move to table the bill.

related.title: Pastor and Gubernatorial Candidate Dan Fisher Responds to Oklahoma Baptist Leaders Opposing SB13
related.reason: Provides the church-political context referenced when the article cites the Oklahoma Baptists' resolution demanding SB13.

related.title: The Three Pro-Life Men Keeping Abortion Legal in Oklahoma
related.reason: Earlier Silberman piece identifying the leadership chokepoints (including Treat) blocking SB13, giving context for naming names in this alert.
